programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called to the scene of a fatal car crash involving marine lance corporal Chad Dunn and student Lyla Cutwright. Duckie notices that the woman's injuries do not match the specifics of the accident and there are no skidmarks found at the scene, leading Gibbs to declare they possibly have a murder and a suicide on their hands. Abby calls in sick for the first time after the case stirs up a traumatic incident from her childhood. Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette star(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team capture computer hacker Ajay Khan, who was recently responsible for hacking into NCIS's supposedly ultra-secure multiple-threat alert centre. They believe he could hold the key to tracking down the most-wanted cyber terrorist in the world, who may be plotting an attack using a bomb containing a strain of the Ebola virus. When Khan refuses to co-operate, Ziva and DiNozzo take him to Guantanamo Bay to be placed with people he had previously betrayed. Guest starring Vik Sahay (Chuck)(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the case of a marine who dropped dead while scaling a wall on an assault course, and a fellow soldier reveals that he was heavily involved with an underground fight club, where competitors would battle for cash. Meanwhile, having returned to work after compassionate leave, Vance discovers paperwork and a key in his late wife's effects that lead him to a safety deposit box containing a stash of cash(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While transporting the corpse of Lt Gordon Roth to the morgue, a flat tyre brings Ducky and Jimmy to a halt and they are abducted and taken to a remote cabin - where one of their captors wants the post-mortem to take place. Back at the scene, Abby finds a blood sample that does not belong to the victim, who in fact bore little resemblance to the picture of Roth in his old school yearbook. Guest starring Valerie Cruz (The Following)(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A woman is stabbed to death after stepping out of her shower, becoming the latest victim of the Dead Rose Slasher. Gibbs is approached by his old friend and barber Frankie Dean, who shares his concern that his son Cameron may be the killer. He explains that a photo-fit released to the media resembles him, and the attacks stopped during a period coinciding with his three-year stint in the navy. Meanwhile, Tony has to take probationary agent Ned Dorneget under his wing(n)

programme.title: 48 Hours: Mystery

programme.title: 48 Hours: Mystery
programme.desc: The inspiring story of a girl's will to live, her unwavering focus on pushing police to find her kidnapper, and how she found a purpose in life by advocating for other victims(n)

However, Ziva and the team discover her supposed assailant actually died of a heart attack days before the crime was committed. A lead from Barrett's squad reveals the CIA has also been tracking the murderer - and its findings suggest the culprit could be an even deadlier foe than anticipated(n)

programme.desc: Gibbs and the team attend the funeral of a fallen colleague before resuming their hunt for the man they believe to be the port-to-port killer. programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the wildlife of Europe, a crowded continent hiding the most surprising animals in pockets of wilderness. Gibraltar Nature Reserve is home to Europe's only wild population of primates, with its Barbary macaques living a life of kidnapping and high drama, while in the cemeteries of Vienna graverobbing European hamsters do battle with one another. Italian mountain villages are the hunting grounds for rarely seen wolves, and lynx lurk in the forests of Spain(n)

programme.title: Countryfile

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in North America, which is defined by extreme weather and seasonal change - from tornados that roar across the prairies, to arctic air sweeping through the humid, southern swamps. In winter, lynx prowl the snowy Yukon for snowshoe hares, while Florida manatees seek hot springs to escape the freeze, while in summer fireflies light up the forests at night. Plus, footage of roadrunners cruising the spectacular deserts of Arizona, and polar bears leaping from rocks to hunt beluga whales(n)

programme.title: The War of the Worlds
programme.desc: Amy is reunited with Ogilvy and they piece together the events of the invasion hoping to find the clue to the Martians' weakness. But in order to unlock the key to the human fightback, she must face traumatising memories. Coming round from his delerium in a butchery where he has taken shelter, George is lucid enough to realise he needs to save Amy and his unborn child and makes a desperate decision. Adaptation of HG Wells' sci-fi classic, starring Rafe Spall, Eleanor Tomlinson , Rupert Graves and Robert Carlyle(n)

But when Lisa tries to confront the woman she blames for her friend's death, she is stopped by Louise. Chantelle is stunned when Gray opens up about his past to the therapist, Billy hatches a plan to solve Karen's money problems and Mick thinks of a new way to curb Linda's drinking(n)

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner invites satirist Charlie Brooker, presenter Scarlett Moffatt and actress Pearl Mackie to express their anger over individuals, events and objects that upset them, hoping to curry the host's favour and see them consigned to oblivion. Their gripes include crocodiles, mosquitoes and having one's hair cut(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: As Prince Andrew steps back from his public duties, Darragh MacIntyre hears from the victims of is former friend and convicted child sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. The Duke of York has been widely criticised for not expressing sympathy for these women during his television interview with the BBC's Newsnight programme. Virginia Roberts Giuffre claims she was trafficked by Epstein and forced to have sex with the prince, something he denies. In her first interview on British TV, she tells her story and reveals new details about her time with Epstein. Reporter Darragh MacIntyre also looks at another of the duke's friends, Ghislaine Maxwell, and investigates her alleged role in Epstein's prolific sex offending. As the FBI investigation continues, Panorama asks what next in the scandal of Epstein and the prince?(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock and Mairead come up with a scheme to make money and buy a second-hand chip van, persuading Mairead to come on board with the venture on the understanding that she will provide the fish. huge football match in Cork provides them with an opportunity to make a killing financially, but luck doesn't always go their way(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60 on a Mission
programme.desc: Steve Backshall tracks down the deadliest animals in Asia, beginning in Indonesia where he and his crew are chased by a group of ravenous Komodo dragons, the world's most venomous lizard. In Nepal, he tracks down a grumpy rhinoceros and stalks the forests in search of the elusive Bengal tiger - the largest cat on Earth - before paragliding alongside an Egyptian vulture above the foothills of the Himalayas(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Port Lockroy on Goudier Island in the British Antarctic Territory is the site of one of the world's most remote post offices, which has a staff of four and is open to visiting tourists during the Antarctic summer. They share the neighbourhood with more than 3,000 gentoo penguins, and this documentary follows the trials and tribulations of the birds as they try to raise their chicks in these unusual surroundings. Juliet Stevenson narrates(n)

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara Ó Briain and Angela Scanlon present the first group heat, with eight teams facing off through two group battles in the arena for a place in the grand final. A new twist on the format sees random house robots being sent to attack the competitors if they stray into the wrong part of the arena. Jonathan Pearce commentates on the action, while Professor Noel Sharkey, Dr Lucy Rogers and Professor Sethu Vijayakumar judge the operators' skills and select a victor in the case of an uncertain outcome(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Former Love Island stars Wes Nelson and Georgia Steel join experts Izzie Balmer and Philip Serrell in North Yorkshire on a quest for items to sell at an auction in North Shields. On a break from antique hunting, Wes takes Izzie to Scarborough Spa to hear about the resort's remarkable history, while the other pair go for a trip on a very old vessel(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace presents the final week of the heats. For the Skills Test, Monica Galetti challenges three chefs to make a braised artichoke with saffron aquafaba mayonnaise, and Marcus Wareing sets three more the task of making chicken Schnitzel Holstein. All six contenders go through to the Signature round, which will determine who wins a place in the quarter final(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: In the last heat of the competition, the first three chefs recreate Monica's dish of oeufs meurette, before the next three take on Marcus' drop scones and a suzette sauce. In the next round, the six chefs are given a chance to showcase their creativity and cooking style as they present a dish of their own design to the judges(n)

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: A family home with an industrial twist, a sleek, split-level semi-detached property, and an eclectic Edwardian abode with a menagerie of surprises are all judged by neighbours(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Extraordinary Homes
programme.desc: Piers Taylor and Caroline Quentin explore unusual homes built underground. In Greece, they view a house hidden beneath the landscape that still boasts stunning sea views. In the Swiss Alps, they visit a house made so invisible it has to be accessed via a tunnel. Next it's over to New Zealand's South Island to a house built underground to soften its impact on the landscape as well as withstand the threat of earthquakes, before the pair inspect a Dutch house created by deep excavation, which features a huge, light-filled open-plan living space(n)

programme.title: Radio 1 Teen Awards Highlights 2019
programme.desc: Greg James, Maya Jama and Mollie King host the ceremony that celebrates the achievements of inspirational teenagers alongside awards for leading stars from the music, sport and entertainment worlds. Including performances by Yungblud, AJ Tracey, Jax Jones and Ella Henderson(n)

programme.title: Football: Euro 2020 Draw
programme.desc: Mark Chapman introduces coverage of the event in Bucharest, Romania, as the 20 countries who have already qualified for next summer's tournament discover their opponents. The Championship is scheduled to be held across 12 European cities to celebrate the 60th birthday of the competition, with Wembley Stadium set to host three group games, one last-16 tie, both semi-finals and the final(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: A secret weapon goes haywire and threatens Walmington-on-Sea, so Mainwaring and his platoon abandon their cookhouse duties and speed to the rescue in Corporal Jones's van. Comedy, starring Arthur Lowe and Clive Dunn. First shown in 1972(n)

programme.title: Wales: Land of the Wild
programme.desc: Summer arrives and the country is basking in a heatwave, a time for some of Wales's smaller - or more exotic - wild animals to shine. On Gronant beach in Flintshire, 350 female terns spread out across the sand to lay their eggs, while hungry gulls circle above. Meanwhile, thousands of crabs gather in the warm waters of the west to shed their shells and on the island of Skokholm, rabbits emerge from their warrens. But they, too, are at risk from predators(n)

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels along the eastern coast of Australia from the coal mines of Newcastle, New South Wales, to Queensland's modern state capital, Brisbane. In the world's largest coal-exporting port, Michael marvels at the skill of the loader as he transfers thousands of tons of coal from rail to ship. He visits the Koala Hospital at Port Macquarie, where he finds populations of these marsupials are in rapid decline. Michael also visits a factory where the quintessential Australian bush hat is made and in Woolgoolga, he joins a Sikh community to hear how they have integrated into Australian society(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig looks at 'quacks' and 'quackery', with regular panellist Alan Davies and guest players Victoria Coren Mitchell, Stephen K Amos and Claudia Winkleman. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Seamus Heaney and the Music of What Happens
programme.desc: Documentary about the life and work of Irish poet Seamus Heaney, with his widow Marie and children Michael, Christopher and Catherine talking about their family life and reading some of the poems he wrote for them. His surviving brothers Hugh, Charlie, Colum and Dan remember their childhood and the shared experiences that inspired many of his finest poems(n)

programme.title: Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine presents highlights of the fourth day at the Barbican Centre in York, which featured matches in the last 64 of the prestigious ranking tournament(n)

programme.title: Snooker: UK Championship Extra
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine presents extended highlights from one of today's matches at the Barbican Centre in York, where the last 64 got under way(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Loose Women panellists Andrea McLean and Penny Lancaster search the antiques shops of Essex in the hope of making a profit, with expert guidance from auctioneer Tim Medhust and dealer David Harper. Penny is already a keen collector and even finds the time to pick up a gift for husband Rod Stewart. How will newbie Andrea compare against her more experienced colleague?(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and expert designer Danny Clarke head to Basingstoke, Hampshire, to breathe new life into mother-of-five Tania's disorganised garden and turn it into a child-safe and enchanting space(n)

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Gardens
programme.desc: Armed with a long-standing interest in Zen Buddhism, Monty Don takes his globe-trotting tour to the Orient, where he investigates two very different approaches to gardening in China and Japan. His research leads him first to China's ancient scholars' gardens in Suzhou, and after gaining a new insight into oriental horticulture, he journeys to Kyoto, the Japanese city of gardens(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker heads to the heart of the Snowdonia National Park to explore its Slate Trail, an 83-mile loop taking in some of the area's least-trodden path, while Ellie Harrison is in the foothills of Llanberis to find about about the Outdoor Partnership. Plus, Tom Heap investigates the impact of falling farm gate prices on the British beef industry(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The six strongest chefs compete in the last quarter-final, inventing a dish that uses squash as the key ingredient. After the weakest two are sent home, the others cook two courses for three critics - Jay Rayner, Jimi Famurewa and Tracey MacLeod. However, judges Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing will ultimately decide who deserves a place in Knockout Week(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick's tour brings him to Perigord, which is famous for its black truffles and walnuts, as well as duck dishes. He pays a visit to his favourite market in the city of Perigueux, and traces the historic connections between England and the city, which was once known as the Kingdom of Aquitaine. Inspired by his visit, he returns to the kitchen to prepare Enchaud, a traditional pot roast pork from the region, before heading towards the shores of the Mediterranean and the Cote Catalan near the Spanish border(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the fifth day at the Barbican Centre in York, featuring matches in the last 64 of the prestigious ranking tournament. All contests in this stage of the competition will be played over the best off 11 frames, with the winners progressing to the last 32, which begins tomorrow. Hazel Irvine presents, with commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, Dennis Taylor and John Virgo(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Manish Bhasin presents action from today's second-round fixtures, including Tranmere Rovers or Wycombe Wanderers v Chichester City. The visitors, who play in the Isthmian League South East Division were the lowest-ranked team remaining in the competition and received a first-round bye. With analysis by Dion Dublin and Kevin Kilbane(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness present an electric cars special, each taking up the challenge of constructing a battery-powered sports car on a budget. Plus, Chris takes the Tesla Model 3 for a spin on the test track. Plus, Danny Boyle and Himesh Patel, director and star of fantasy comedy Yesterday, drop into the studio(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Deborah Meaden, Peter Jones, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Jenny Campbell assess the profit-making potential of a space age business venture created by two scientists, an African-inspired range of skincare products, and an online pub quiz app created by a Leeds-based entrepreneur. Evan Davis presents(n)

programme.title: Reggie in China
programme.desc: Reggie Yates visits Shanghai and Chengdu in search of underground Chinese culture, meeting the artists who are pushing the limits of government censorship. He discovers how the Chinese Communist Party has capitalised on the popularity of hip-hop to promote their own agenda, and investigates how Western influences have found a foothold in the country in recent years, with both positive and negative consequences(n)

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: The comedian is in the Canadian Arctic for this festive special of the travelogue in which he explores countries rarely visited by tourists. He wants to know whether there is more to this area than the prospect of freezing to death or being eaten by a polar bear. Travelling across frozen wastelands with host Johnny Issaluk, he spends a week in the largest territory, getting to grips with the Inuit hunter's way of life - not an easy task for vegan Romesh. He also witnesses the erosion and resurgence of Inuit traditions and discover why alcohol is often restricted in the communities(n)

programme.title: The Program (2015)
programme.desc: A journalist follows Lance Armstrong's rise to dominate the world of professional cycling, even following a successful battle with cancer. As time goes on, he begins to suspect the champion is boosting his performance with drugs, and in the face of condemnation from the media, sets out to expose the truth. Fact-based drama, starring Ben Foster, Chris O'Dowd and Dustin Hoffman(n)

programme.title: Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine presents highlights of the fifth day at the Barbican Centre in York, which featured the concluding matches in the last 64 of the prestigious ranking tournament(n)

programme.title: Snooker: UK Championship Extra
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine presents extended highlights from one of today's matches at the Barbican Centre in York, where the last 64 was brought to a conclusion(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three semi-detached properties in Leicester - including a shabby chic family home, and an Edwardian property with some rather eclectic interiors - are in the spotlight(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein examine the 1935 morphine poisoning of a care home resident Ada Baguley in Nottingham. Initially Ada was thought to have died from natural causes, but a post-mortem revealed her body to contain lethal doses of morphine. Dorothea Waddingham, the owner of the nursing home and primary care giver of Ada, was later convicted of murder(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metal expert Dom Chinea and wood restorer Will Kirk team up to tackle the restoration of a treasured steering wheel from a scrapped Scottish fishing boat. A worn-out Portsmouth football club mascot and a vintage cricket ball are also revived. Presented by Jay Blades(n)

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: It's been raining for days and vet Maurice King is called into the practice car park to examine a newborn lamb that's in danger of succumbing to hypothermia. The birthing season in the Mournes is reaching its peak as Rachael Frew determines a heavily pregnant ewe must undergo an emergency C-section. Meanwhile, Flame the dachshund and Monty the fell pony present worrying symptoms that put the vets' knowledge to the test. Narrated by Kevin Murphy(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the sixth day at the Barbican Centre in York, featuring best-of-11-frame matches in the last 32 of the prestigious ranking tournament. Eventual champion Ronnie O'Sullivan was at his unstoppable best at this stage last year, whitewashing Zhou Yuelong 6-0. Hazel Irvine presents, with commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, Dennis Taylor and John Virgo(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal is joined by the latest couple to be eliminated from the competition after losing the dance-off. Plus, in-depth analysis of the weekend's performances(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents live coverage of the draw from Etihad Stadium, home of holders Manchester City, as the remaining teams await their fate. The lower-league sides to have progressed this far will be hoping to receive a money-spinning draw against one of the 44 Premier League and Championship clubs entering the competition at this stage(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the unpaid volunteers who sacrifice their free time to staff more than two hundred RNLI lifeboat stations across the UK(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts the opening second-round match as the Lexplorers take on the Suits for a place in the quarter-finals. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Green iguana, Eurasian lynx, American bison and Western gorilla(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz continues, with two more teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure a place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: Vienna Blood
programme.desc: As Max tells his family that Clara has broken off their engagement, the Liebermanns are shocked by an act of violence committed by Leah's son Daniel. When bruises are found on the boy's body, Max persuades Oskar that the military academy his nephew attends should be investigated. The learn that one of the pupils died in an accident 10 days previously and when Max finds a coded note in Daniel's things he begins to suspect some sinister goings on at the academy. Crime drama, starring Matthew Beard(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents action from this evening's last-32 matches on the sixth day at the Barbican Centre in York(n)

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham investigates unusual natural events, with the help of eyewitness accounts, scientists and footage of the occurrences(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary following Baghani, a tigress chosen to breed in the wild. She is airlifted from her home in an Indian park to a new reserve where she encounters Rajore, a male who has also recently been released. The pair are filmed over two years, as they fight leopards for territory and learn to hunt boar, while trying to avoid being killed by poachers(n)

programme.title: The Lake District: A Wild Year
programme.desc: For the wildlife and people who live amid the stunning scenery of the Lake District, life is one of continuous change. This documentary features cutting-edge camera techniques offering a new and unique perspective on a turbulent year in the life of England's largest national park. Time-lapse photography shows months and weeks passing in seconds - snow and ice giving way to sunshine or the frequent rain showers - while the animals, plants and people find extraordinary ways to cope with the challenges of this ancient landscape. Narrated by Bernard Cribbins(n)

programme.title: The Sinner
programme.desc: Vera believes that Harry is behind the arrest of Julian, who has been taken into custody at a juvenile detention centre and charged with second-degree murder. Heather calls on Mrs Calhoun to question her about the disappearance of her daughter Marin. Crime drama, starring Bill Pullman, Natalie Paul and Carrie Coon(n)

programme.title: The Sinner
programme.desc: Harry receives a phone call from Julian, who doubts that Vera will be able to get him released from detention. The detective then returns to Mosswood to discover more about the commune's work. Crime drama, starring Bill Pullman and Carrie Coon(n)

programme.title: Radio 2 in Concert
programme.desc: Jo Whiley introduces a gig filmed at the BBC Radio Theatre in London, with the singer and his band performing songs from new album From Out of Nowhere as well as classic tracks including Evil Woman, Livin' Thing' and Mr Blue Sky(n)

programme.title: Jeff Lynne's ELO - Wembley or Bust
programme.desc: A 2017 concert at Wembley Stadium in which the musician and producer was joined by his ensemble band to deliver a show filled with vintage ELO tunes such as Mr Blue Sky, Livin' Thing, and Evil Woman. Jeff also performs Do Ya from his days with the Move, as well as Handle With Care, which he recorded with the Traveling Wilburys(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Susie Mathis present the edition first aired on November 17, featuring Yazz, All About Eve, INXS, Mica Paris, Tanita Tikaram, Robin Beck and Salt-N-Pepa(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Simon Mayo and Andy Crane present the edition first aired on November 24, featuring performances by Tiffany, Bomb the Bass featuring Maureen, Deacon Blue, Michael Jackson, Pet Shop Boys, Iron Maiden, Bananarama, Chris de Burgh, Robin Beck and Hithouse(n)

programme.title: The Lake District: A Wild Year
programme.desc: For the wildlife and people who live amid the stunning scenery of the Lake District, life is one of continuous change. This documentary features cutting-edge camera techniques offering a new and unique perspective on a turbulent year in the life of England's largest national park. Time-lapse photography shows months and weeks passing in seconds - snow and ice giving way to sunshine or the frequent rain showers - while the animals, plants and people find extraordinary ways to cope with the challenges of this ancient landscape. Narrated by Bernard Cribbins(n)

programme.title: Music Moguls: Masters of Pop
programme.desc: The secret history of pop and rock from the men and women who pull the strings behind the scenes. The first of three programmes tells the story of the maverick managers who controlled the careers of major artists, from Colonel Tom Parker (Elvis) up to Scooter Braun (Justin Bieber). Along the way are tales of industry legends such as Led Zeppelin's Peter Grant, and Don Arden, who managed the Small Faces, Black Sabbath and ELO. Contributors include Andrew Loog Oldham, Jon Landau, Bill Curbishley, Paul McGuinness and Jonathan Dickins(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest games in the Super League, including Everton v Chelsea at Haig Avenue, Manchester City v Liverpool at PTS Academy Stadium and Arsenal v Bristol City at Meadow Park(n)

programme.title: The River Taff with Will Millard
programme.desc: Writer and fisherman Will Millard goes wild swimming with a group of eccentrics trying to change the image of the often forgotten waterway. He meets retired Somali sailors drawn to Cardiff in the city's glory days as a thriving port and tells the story of how the industrial docks have been transformed. Finally, he sets out to catch migratory salmon returning to the place where they were born(n)

programme.title: Fair Isle: Living on the Edge
programme.desc: Part two of two. Eleven-year-old Ythan and his family learn to deal with his homesickness, while new inhabitants Shaun and Rachel decide whether to stay on after their first winter(n)

programme.title: Gauguin - A Dangerous Life
programme.desc: A biography of Paul Gauguin, a committed multi-media artist who worked with the Impressionists and had a tempestuous relationship with Vincent van Gogh. He left his wife to bring up five children and used his colonial privilege to travel to Polynesia, where in his 40s he took 'wives' aged 13-15, creating images of them and their world that promoted a fantasy paradise of an unspoilt Eden in the Pacific(n)

programme.title: Charles I: Downfall of a King
programme.desc: With the aid of dramatic reconstructions, historian Lisa Hilton discovers how in just 50 tempestuous days Charles I's rule collapsed, laying the foundations for civil war, the loss of Royal power and ultimately, the King's head(n)

programme.title: Charles I: Downfall of a King
programme.desc: John Pym tries to pass parliamentary bills limiting the King's power, but in the Lords, the casting votes are held by the bishops, loyal to Charles. Pym knows that to succeed he must remove them, and they become his next target. In mid-December, his group goes for a political ambush, demanding that the Grand Remonstrance be published. They launch a surprise vote in the dead of night and win. Making matters worse for the King, rumours spread that the Queen was involved in the Irish rebellion(n)

programme.title: Charles I: Downfall of a King
programme.desc: Documentary drama charting the collapse of Charles I's rule in just 50 tempestuous days. Lisa Hilton relates how, after John Pym spurned Charles I's attempt to bring him onside with a job offer, the king was left with just one option - to arrest Pym and his colleagues, accusing them of high treason. But in his attempt to draw the politician out, the king played right into his enemy's hands, appearing like a tyrant to all of Westminster(n)

programme.title: Naples '44
programme.desc: A portrait of the city at the end of the Second World War, using British intelligence operative Norman Lewis' first-hand accounts, read by Benedict Cumberbatch. They describe a city devastated by Fascism, bombings, Nazi occupation and the Allied invasion, combined with archive footage of Naples in later years, as life returned to the city(n)

programme.title: Gauguin - A Dangerous Life
programme.desc: A biography of Paul Gauguin, a committed multi-media artist who worked with the Impressionists and had a tempestuous relationship with Vincent van Gogh. He left his wife to bring up five children and used his colonial privilege to travel to Polynesia, where in his 40s he took 'wives' aged 13-15, creating images of them and their world that promoted a fantasy paradise of an unspoilt Eden in the Pacific(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar seeks inspiration as she explores Wensleydale, setting out from Leyburn on a walk to Bolton Castle, where Mary, Queen of Scots was imprisoned in 1568. Along the way, Shanaz discovers evidence of an industrial past and talks to a volunteer worker on the heritage railway line, while also stopping to sketch and reflect(n)

programme.title: Vienna: Empire, Dynasty and Dream
programme.desc: Simon Sebag Montefiore examines the transformation of Vienna into a great cultural capital after the Ottoman threat receded at the end of the 17th century. The city, no longer an outpost defending the West from Islamic invaders, was rebuilt and the Habsburgs vowed their empire and imperial capital would become the most glittering in the world, a magnet for musicians including Mozart, Haydn and Beethoven(n)

programme.title: Pleasure and Pain with Michael Mosley
programme.desc: The journalist examines why pleasure and pain are integral to human survival, submitting himself to some of the most extreme forms of both sensations. He reveals how the hottest chilli in the world creates a sense of euphoria in the brain, and explains the biological reasons why parents feel a surge of love for newborn children. He also exposes himself to painful experiments, highlighting the dangers of life without discomfort and discovering how far he is prepared to go to experience one of the world's most pleasurable highs(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Filmed over six years, this documentary reveals the struggle of victims of Spain's 40-year dictatorship under General Franco. It follows them as they organise a groundbreaking international lawsuit and fight a government- sanctioned 'pact of forgetting' relating to the crimes they suffered(n)

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: Series examining how the BBC has revealed and interpreted moments in history, as well as looking at changes in research covered in documentary television. In the first edition, Professor Alice Roberts uses 70 years of BBC history archive to uncover how Stonehenge has been explained, argued over and debated by some of Britain's leading historians and archaeologists. She looks at how new theories discredited old ones, how astronomers and geologists became involved, and why, even after centuries of study, there is still no definitive answer to the mystery of Stonehenge(n)

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: When Francine needs money for new soccer cleats, Muffy hires her as a personal assistant. However, it remains to be seen whether drinking pumpkin smoothies and playing games with a best friend the best way to make an honest buck(n)

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: The farm is transformed into a chilled environment where work and chores are abolished and the animals are encouraged to express themselves(n)

programme.desc: Pieface's first sleepover in the den is troubled by spooky tales of a frightening Fangtom(n)

programme.title: Strange Hill High

programme.title: The Zoo
programme.desc: It's time for the zoo's new tiger cub to meet his massive, meat-munching, food-obsessed dad. As rumours of the big event spread around the zoo, its residents gather to watch the showdown. Self-appointed leader and lifestyle guru, Jurgen, offers some sage advice to the tiny tiger cub, until he too gets spooked by the prospect of facing such a big predator. With only his courage to drawn on, will the diminutive cub find his inner tiger in time?(n)

programme.desc: A new teacher knows about the headmaster's past - but can the gang trust her?(n)

programme.desc: Granny Bob arrives on her motorbike, declaring she will whip the kids into shape(n)

programme.title: The Dumping Ground
programme.desc: The everyday lives and experiences of children living in a fictional care home. Drama based on books by Jacqueline Wilson, starring Connor Byrne and Kay Purcell(n)

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Joshua, 11, and his younger brother Owen are great friends, but Owen has Downs syndrome, which means that a lot of his parents' time is taken up looking after him. Joshua loves DJ'ing and admires Radio 1's Annie Mac. Here, helpers make Joshua his very own DJ den(n)

programme.title: Holly Hobbie
programme.desc: Worried that she might not win the Pickle Pageant, Holly struggles to be true to herself.(n)

programme.desc: Holly, Amy and Piper discover that the local pickle factory is polluting the nearby creek.(n)

programme.title: Holly Hobbie
programme.desc: Holly and Piper both plan birthday parties on the same day(n)

programme.title: Holly Hobbie
programme.desc: Holly tries to escape her family drama by running away to Chicago with Tyler(n)

programme.desc: Cobie tells the story of Molly and his best friend Adam, who are both trying to recover from being hit by cars(n)

programme.title: Our School
programme.desc: Following youngsters as they make the transition from primary to secondary school and begin the most important year of their school lives so far(n)

programme.title: Show Me What You're Made Of
programme.desc: Tom, Eloushka, Lucas, Maddie and Madz travel to a massive sandwich factory in London where they make seven hundred thousand sandwiches a week. Adelie Foods has very high hygiene standards and productivity rates and their workers are disciplined and incredibly fast. Will our five being able to demonstrate the focus needed to succeed here? When the boss starts them off making a cheese and pickle ploughman's sandwich, it becomes clear that this work is considerably more difficult than they had anticipated. As they all compete for a promotion, who has the skill to keep up with the production lines and make sandwiches of the quality that their boss expects?(n)

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Dr Xand visits the largest blood factory in the world and donates his blood to see how it is processed, to help save lives. Dr Chris meets the Midlands air ambulance crew(n)

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: Shaun persuades Bitzer to take part in his game of cricket, but the resident moles are unhappy about it taking place above their heads and a full scale war ensues(n)

Italian mountain villages are the hunting grounds for rarely seen wolves, and lynx lurk in the forests of Spain(n)

In winter, lynx prowl the snowy Yukon for snowshoe hares, while Florida manatees seek hot springs to escape the freeze, while in summer fireflies light up the forests at night. Plus, footage of roadrunners cruising the spectacular deserts of Arizona, and polar bears leaping from rocks to hunt beluga whales(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Interviews

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The day of Mel's funeral arrives and everyone gathers to remember their friend - although Lisa's mood darkens when Phil and Sharon turn up at the wake. But when Lisa tries to confront the woman she blames for her friend's death, she is stopped by Louise. Chantelle is stunned when Gray opens up about his past to the therapist, Billy hatches a plan to solve Karen's money problems and Mick thinks of a new way to curb Linda's drinking(n)

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: Visitors to the Royal Welsh Winter Fair test unusual Christmas drinks and Rachel Treadaway-Williams investigates a company failing on its promise of taking the hassle out of tax returns. Omar Hamdi meets a man whose finances were hacked by fraudsters to fund an online gaming habit, while Lucy Owen visits a garage well-known to the programme(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: As Prince Andrew steps back from his public duties, Darragh MacIntyre hears from the victims of is former friend and convicted child sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. The Duke of York has been widely criticised for not expressing sympathy for these women during his television interview with the BBC's Newsnight programme. Virginia Roberts Giuffre claims she was trafficked by Epstein and forced to have sex with the prince, something he denies. In her first interview on British TV, she tells her story and reveals new details about her time with Epstein. Reporter Darragh MacIntyre also looks at another of the duke's friends, Ghislaine Maxwell, and investigates her alleged role in Epstein's prolific sex offending. As the FBI investigation continues, Panorama asks what next in the scandal of Epstein and the prince?(n)

programme.title: Tourist Trap
programme.desc: The agency decides to hold an awards ceremony and manages to book TV and radio presenter Jason Mohammad to host it. Elaine decides to read a poem in Welsh at the event, but her failure to have it translated properly before delivering it leaves the audience in shock(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock and Mairead come up with a scheme to make money and buy a second-hand chip van, persuading Mairead to come on board with the venture on the understanding that she will provide the fish. huge football match in Cork provides them with an opportunity to make a killing financially, but luck doesn't always go their way(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Peaky Blinders star Helen McCrory hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with stand-up comedian Chris McCausland one of two guests joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In the third episode, mother and daughter Janette and Lynsey from Wigton, Cumbria, friends Ross and John from Bristol, and sisters Natalie and Christina from Bedfordshire take part(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: It's Musicals Week on Strictly, meaning that remaining contestants Alex, Chris, Karim, Kelvin and Emma - minus Saffron, who was voted off last week - are keen to dazzle as they bring a taste of Broadway to the studio. However, it's also the quarter-final and with the final looming ever closer, the pressure is on. The extravaganza gets under way with a big opening number featuring none other than Craig Revel Horwood. Presented Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: The lively comedian is joined by guests Emma Bunton and Judge Rinder for an evening of entertainment, recorded live in front of an audience at the London Palladium. Spice Girl and presenter Emma hands over her phone to play Send To All, and two brothers from Coventry are in for the shock of their lives when they find themselves on stage as they become the Unexpected Stars. Plus, Judge Rinder faces a rude awakening in The Midnight Gameshow, and breakthrough singer-songwriter Freya Ridings performs her hit single, Castles(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Charlie is shocked to learn that Duffy's condition means she cannot look after herself, and the pair consider the option of a nursing home to help ease the burden of Duffy's illness. Meanwhile, Rosa and David try to keep their wedding plans under wraps, Rosa finds out that her mother has lied to David, and Mason's behaviour angers Rash(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Brighton & Hove Albion at Anfield and Newcastle United v Manchester City at St James' Park. Jurgen Klopp's Reds came into this contest holding an excellent record against the Seagulls, having won all four of their previous top-flight encounters, including a 1-0 victory in last season's corresponding fixture. Plus, action from Burnley v Crystal Palace at Turf Moor, Chelsea v West Ham United at Stamford Bridge, Tottenham Hotspur v Bournemouth at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium and Southampton v Watford at St Mary's Stadium. With analysis by Phil Neville and Alan Shearer(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Atlanta Falcons v New Orleans Saints at Mercedes-Benz Stadium(n)

programme.title: The Uninvited (2009)
programme.desc: A teenager is left traumatised by her mother's death in a fire, and is committed to a mental institution after trying to kill herself. A year later, she is released and reunited with her sister, but they both begin to suspect their new stepmother may have been responsible for the fire. Thriller, starring Emily Browning, Arielle Kebbel, David Strathairn and Elizabeth Banks(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad introduces continuous updates from today's matches in the FA Cup second round, with goals shown from selected ties as soon as they are scored. With analysis by Dion Dublin and Jon Walters(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the wildlife of Europe, a crowded continent hiding the most surprising animals in pockets of wilderness. Gibraltar Nature Reserve is home to Europe's only wild population of primates, with its Barbary macaques living a life of kidnapping and high drama, while in the cemeteries of Vienna graverobbing European hamsters do battle with one another. Italian mountain villages are the hunting grounds for rarely seen wolves, and lynx lurk in the forests of Spain(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Helen Skelton explore the Tees Valley, while Tom Heap investigates whether enough is being done to protect UK farming and food production from flooding(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in North America, which is defined by extreme weather and seasonal change - from tornados that roar across the prairies, to arctic air sweeping through the humid, southern swamps. In winter, lynx prowl the snowy Yukon for snowshoe hares, while Florida manatees seek hot springs to escape the freeze, while in summer fireflies light up the forests at night. Plus, footage of roadrunners cruising the spectacular deserts of Arizona, and polar bears leaping from rocks to hunt beluga whales(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results, leaving the two couples with the lowest combined scores to take part in a dance-off to decide who will be next to leave the contest before next week's semi-final. With performances by soul singer Beverley Knight and Broadway superstar Idina Menzel(n)

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: The alethiometer sends Lyra and Iorek on a new path, leading to a shocking but vital clue in her search to find her friend Roger and the other missing children. Adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen, Anne-Marie Duff and James Cosmo(n)

programme.title: The War of the Worlds
programme.desc: Amy is reunited with Ogilvy and they piece together the events of the invasion hoping to find the clue to the Martians' weakness. But in order to unlock the key to the human fightback, she must face traumatising memories. Coming round from his delerium in a butchery where he has taken shelter, George is lucid enough to realise he needs to save Amy and his unborn child and makes a desperate decision. Adaptation of HG Wells' sci-fi classic, starring Rafe Spall, Eleanor Tomlinson , Rupert Graves and Robert Carlyle(n)

programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a buyer find a new home in Truro, Cornwall on a £260,000 budget, as well as investigating a campaign by surfers and divers to clean up the oceans(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers help neighbours near Manchester who have decided to combine their respective plots into one garden now they have become a couple(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore three more family heirlooms, taking on a much loved-teddy bear, an unusual instrument with a music hall past and a tea caddy that is a century old(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.desc: By the Democratic Unionist Party(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The day of Mel's funeral arrives and everyone gathers to remember their friend - although Lisa's mood darkens when Phil and Sharon turn up at the wake. But when Lisa tries to confront the woman she blames for her friend's death, she is stopped by Louise. Chantelle is stunned when Gray opens up about his past to the therapist, Billy hatches a plan to solve Karen's money problems and Mick thinks of a new way to curb Linda's drinking(n)

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner invites satirist Charlie Brooker, presenter Scarlett Moffatt and actress Pearl Mackie to express their anger over individuals, events and objects that upset them, hoping to curry the host's favour and see them consigned to oblivion. Their gripes include crocodiles, mosquitoes and having one's hair cut(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: As Prince Andrew steps back from his public duties, Darragh MacIntyre hears from the victims of is former friend and convicted child sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. The Duke of York has been widely criticised for not expressing sympathy for these women during his television interview with the BBC's Newsnight programme. Virginia Roberts Giuffre claims she was trafficked by Epstein and forced to have sex with the prince, something he denies. In her first interview on British TV, she tells her story and reveals new details about her time with Epstein. Reporter Darragh MacIntyre also looks at another of the duke's friends, Ghislaine Maxwell, and investigates her alleged role in Epstein's prolific sex offending. As the FBI investigation continues, Panorama asks what next in the scandal of Epstein and the prince?(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock and Mairead come up with a scheme to make money and buy a second-hand chip van, persuading Mairead to come on board with the venture on the understanding that she will provide the fish. huge football match in Cork provides them with an opportunity to make a killing financially, but luck doesn't always go their way(n)

programme.title: BBC Arts NI Presents
programme.desc: Stephen McCauley presents fresh music talent including acoustic sounds by JC Stewart, R&B-infused beats from Dena Anuksa, electro-pop from Alice La, alt-rock from New Pagans and Wild Youth, plus rising stars from the classical world(n)

programme.desc: The potato says goodbye to sweet food(n)

programme.title: Sealgairean Spòrsail (History Hunters)
programme.desc: A look at the Age of Exploration, featuring with fun facts, animations and experiments which show how inventions and discoveries from ancient times helped shape life today(n)

programme.title: An Lot (The Croft)
programme.desc: Sweeny reflects on how the second year on the croft has gone and reveals another new venture which might help to achieve his goal of crofting full-time(n)

programme.title: Speaking Our Language

programme.title: Dealbhan Fraoich (Heather's Portraits)
programme.desc: Heather Dewar draws a colour portrait of storyteller and traveller Essie Stewart, who lives in the village of Bonar Bridge(n)

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: A look at the remarkable work of world-renowned genealogist Bill Lawson from Harris. The wealth of his work over the years includes local village and crofting histories(n)

programme.title: TradFest
programme.desc: Allan MacDonald introduces performances by Aoife Scott & Andy Meaney, I Draw Slow and the Dublin Lasses from the Dublin music festival(n)

programme.title: Opry an Iúir
programme.desc: Daniel O'Donnell returns to the Millennium Forum in Derry to present a special programme with acclaimed Scottish singer Isla Grant(n)

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean explores one of the biggest and deepest lochs in Scotland - Loch Morar(n)

programme.title: An Sgrùdaire
programme.desc: Ruairidh Alasdair explores the question of trans people in professional sport, talking to athletes and campaigners and questioning what he future might hold(n)

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr Bean is on his way to the seaside when the mini runs out of petrol. Undeterred he manages to get a push, but for some reason the do-gooders don't want to push him all the way to the beach!(n)

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Dave is given a bulb to grow. Popping it in his lunch box, it isn't long before he discovers it's partial to Betty's sandwiches.(n)

programme.title: LEGO Jurassic World: The Legend of Isla Nublar
Mighty Magiswords!
programme.desc: Prohyas and Vambre attempt to tame two of their most problematic Magiswords - Taunting Jester and Carnivorous Plant - with the help of Kablammica.(n)

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: The TV chef is joined in the kitchen by husband and wife 1980s singers Martin and Shirlie Kemp, who have recently collaborated on an album of swing covers, as well as chef Stephen Terry and forager Alysia Vasey. Plus, Oz Clarke shares his tips on the best wine for a Christmas party and continues his tour of Britain's best pubs(n)

programme.title: Gino's Italian Escape
programme.desc: For the next leg of his journey, Gino D'Acampo boards an old steam train run by ex-railroad men and volunteers which takes him through the beautiful Tuscan countryside. His first stop is the town of Asciano, where locals try to compete with Siena's famous Palio horse race - only here they ride donkeys. Then it's on to the Chianti region where he visits a vineyard where they don't only make wine, but they also bathe in it too, an ancient tradition dating back to Roman times. After a luxurious wine bath, Gino cooks up a dish fit for any Emperor - farfalle pasta with funghi and spinach(n)

programme.title: Nadia's Family Feasts
programme.desc: Nadia Sawalha re-creates the family feasts of China with chef Jeremy Pang. The menu comprises the street food snack jianbing for breakfast, prawn toasts with smacked cucumber for lunch, Jeremy's claypot chicken and mushroom rice for lunch and Nadia's banana fritters for dessert(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of the 3.00 Ladbrokes Trophy Steeple Chase, and races at 1.50, 2.25 and 3.40 from Newbury, Plus, races at 2.05 and 3.20 from Newcastle. With analysis from Ruby Walsh and AP McCoy, reports by Luke Harvey, Mick Fitzgerald, Alice Plunkett, Rishi Persad, Matt Chapman and Oli Bell, and commentary from Richard Hoiles(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian follows the work of Battersea Dogs & Cats Home in London, meeting staff involved in treating, training and rehoming animals. In the sixth episode, the comedian meets an albino Pekingese riddled with health problems, and in need of an operation on his eye, which is making it difficult for him to find a new home. Meanwhile, a dog rescued from a near death experience in the Thames needs help overcoming his nerves, and a Labrador puppy arrives with a mystery illness affecting his lungs(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as impressionist Rory Bremner, weather reporter Laura Tobin, Dragons' Den's Deborah Meaden and TV gardener and chat show host Alan Titchmarsh pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: The latest group of men to enter the 'love lift' are a Bruno Mars impersonator, a duck lover, a serial dater and a skip driver, who pull out all the stops in the hope of impressing 30 single women and winning a date - but must first get the female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval. Paddy McGuinness presents(n)

programme.title: The X Factor: Celebrity
programme.desc: Dermot O'Leary hosts the climax of the star-studded spin-off from the long-running singing contest. Over the past seven weeks, the line-up of famous faces has been whittled down to today's finalists, all under the watchful mentoring of judging panel Simon Cowell, Nicole Scherzinger and Louis Walsh. In this grand final, they will perform to impress both the viewing public and the judges, with the most popular act being crowned as the first celebrity series champion and securing a recording contract(n)

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity Get Me Out of Here!
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host the latest episode of the celebrity survival challenge, with the campmates facing the prospect of their third week in the jungle, with more tough daily tasks and dreaded Bushtucker Trials ahead of them - and for some, a ticket home(n)

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by guests tennis champion Andy Murray, Luke Evans, recently seen in Second World War drama Midway, doctor-turned-comic and author of bestseller This is Going to Hurt Adam Kay, and comedian Desiree Burch. Plus, one of the biggest and hottest artists in the country, Stormzy, is chatting and performing in the studio(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring a child getting trapped in a deckchair, a cat falling through a ceiling, and a parachutist losing his false teeth(n)

programme.title: Bang on the Money
programme.desc: Rickie Haywood-Williams and Melvin Odoom host the game show in which two teams of friends must battle it out to hit a specific target number 'bang on' in a series of physical and mental challenges. At the end of the contest, the most successful group could win up to £40,000. This week's teams are the Boro Bouncers from Middlesbrough and the Beach Chums from Cleethorpes(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Can Danny handle the pressure of performing basketball tricks in front of hundreds of fans and avoid the messy forfeit?(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The hapless bumbler attempts to make his own home movie, a horror thriller with Mrs Wicket in the starring role. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The hapless bumbler has to look after Mrs Wicket's goldfish, but Scrapper the cat is keen to help. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson(n)

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he's a beach lifeguard but is actually on an ice floe and the swimmers are penguins.(n)

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo's going to miss his flight. Unfortunately, he's not at the airport, but the supermarket, where Fizz and Weasel are hatching their latest plan.(n)

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz is at the beach building a sandcastle. Magoo thinks they are preparing to thwart an attack by an enemy riding a dragon.(n)

programme.title: Be Cool, Scooby-Doo!
programme.desc: Monsters and ghouls get in the way of the Scooby gang's adventures as they travel and spend time together in the summer(n)

programme.title: Robozuna
programme.desc: Children's sci-fi fantasy series about 14-year-old orphan Ariston and his robot Mangle, fighting an evil empire together(n)

programme.title: Spy School
programme.desc: The budding spies are called into action against Goldfist at Chelsea Football Club, and uncover a plot with Eden Hazard himself(n)

programme.title: Beautiful Baking with Juliet Sear
programme.desc: Juliet serves up a selection of bakes that involve spices. She makes spiced pumpkin pie with a meringue topping, chocolate and chilli cookies and winter pavlova with spiced apples and pears. She also visits a Lebanese bakery and is joined by food and travel writer John Gregory-Smith, who makes date and cinnamon squares(n)

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner are starting to feel festive with a few winter warmers. Lisa begins with a blue cheese and butternut squash pie, followed by John's sausage casserole. They also serve a family favourite - macaroni cheese, with a twist - and for dessert it's a classic ginger cake served two ways(n)

programme.title: Nadia's Family Feasts
programme.desc: Nadia Sawalha is joined by Asimakis Chaniotis, London's youngest Michelin-starred chef, to create a family feast from Greece. The menu starts with scrambled eggs for breakfast and chicken gyros for lunch. Then Asimakis rustles up his mum's meatball soup for dinner before Nadia makes Greek almond crescent biscuits for dessert(n)

programme.title: How to Spend It Well at Christmas with Phillip Schofield
programme.desc: Phillip Schofield returns to put more Christmas gifts to the test, aided by his wife Steph, celebrity guests and members of the public. They begin by gearing up for Black Friday with tips on the best gifts and gadgets on the market. With sales of personal electric vehicles on the up, Corrie trio Jack AP Shepherd (David), Alan Halsall (Tyrone) and Colson Smith (Craig) put hoverboards and scooters to the test. Clare Balding is on a mission to find the pefect present for pets and Eddie 'the Eagle' Edwards ranks gift experiences such as track racing, skydiving and flyboarding(n)

programme.title: The X Factor: Celebrity
programme.desc: Dermot O'Leary hosts the climax of the star-studded spin-off from the long-running singing contest. Over the past seven weeks, the line-up of famous faces has been whittled down to today's finalists, all under the watchful mentoring of judging panel Simon Cowell, Nicole Scherzinger and Louis Walsh. In this grand final, they will perform to impress both the viewing public and the judges, with the most popular act being crowned as the first celebrity series champion and securing a recording contract(n)

programme.title: Back to Mine
programme.desc: Globetrotting Emma is house hunting for love with the help of her cousin Kelly - and she has a substantial list of demands to be met as she visits three single men's homes. In the running is a rugby player, a trendy musician and a bodybuilder. Which one will she choose to go back to her house for a date?(n)

programme.title: Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows: Part 2 (2011)
programme.desc: The teenage wizard continues his search for the artefacts containing fragments of the dark lord Voldemort's soul. Time is running out, as the evil sorcerer is aware of Harry's mission, and marshals his forces for a final, devastating attack on Harry and his friends at Hogwarts school. Conclusion of the fantasy saga, with Daniel Radcliffe, Ralph Fiennes, Rupert Grint, Emma Watson and Alan Rickman(n)

programme.title: The ITV Election Debate
programme.desc: Julie Etchingham hosts a debate to which seven parties in the General Election campaign have been invited, ahead of the poll on December 12. The parties are the Conservatives, Labour, the Liberal Democrats, the SNP, the Brexit Party, the Greens and Plaid Cymru. Brexit is likely to be high on the agenda for this debate, along with issues including the NHS, crime, education and climate change(n)

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity Get Me Out of Here!
programme.desc: As the campmates face being booted out of the jungle back into their life of luxury, who is ready to make that jump and who wants to stay another day? Presented by Ant and Dec(n)

programme.title: Holiday Horrors: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston narrates a selection of clips submitted by members of the public, which display in cringe-worthy detail some of the many things that can go wrong while away on holiday. A walk in the woods becomes a fight for survival, a man from Wales becomes a shark-wrestler, and an unfortunate holiday-maker ruins all his once-in-a-lifetime holiday snaps(n)

programme.title: Police, Camera, Auction
programme.desc: An insight into the auctions where criminals' assets from their nefarious activities are sold at auction, with the proceeds usually going to help those whose lives have been blighted by crime. From sports cars and designer goods to outrageous bling and strange collectibles, cameras reveal the broad range of criminals' possessions seized by specialist police teams and put under the hammer. The auctioneers and police discuss their work, while those victims who will benefit from the money wait anxiously to see the outcome of the sales(n)

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents highlights from Brands Hatch, featuring round 22 of the Ginetta GT4 Supercup and round 18 of the Renault UK Clio Cup(n)

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom.(n)

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features(n)

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly(n)

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom.(n)

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions(n)

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and topical studio discussion from a female perspective(n)

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson helps members of the public in Buxton, Derbyshire. Dealer Simon Schneider shows off his ventriloquism skills, and Tim Hogarth is impressed by an unusual telephone(n)

programme.title: The Switch
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Sanjeev Bhaskar in which players four contestants can accumulate big cash totals by answering questions in a series of rounds that alternate between easy and hard, before one gets to the final stage and the chance to double the prize pot(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Pete is stunned by an offer, Diane makes a discovery, and Kim tries to remain composed(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: A vengeful Vicky gatecrashes Robert and Michelle's family gathering, Emma looks forward to her date with Seb, and Roy makes a discovery in Richard's flat(n)

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show
programme.desc: Britain is becoming a nation of switchers, with nearly six million people changing their service suppliers last year. The big six energy companies are now dropping prices - so the demand for better deals is increasing. In this edition devoted to the topic, Martin and Angellica Bell offer advice and reveal the best deals(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: To foil Vicky's threats, Robert signs over his business to Michelle. Meanwhile, Nina rejects Roy's offer of financial assistance, and Daniel fights tears as he watches videos of Sinead(n)

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity Get Me Out of Here!
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host the celebrity survival challenge, which sees a group of famous faces living in the Australian jungle, struggling to complete the tough daily tasks and dreaded Bushtucker Trials. The campmates are tired, hungry and homesick, but need to keep winning stars - and food - if they are going to make it to the end(n)

programme.title: A Very Yorkshire Brothel
programme.desc: Part two of two. Kath and Jenni fly to Germany to see how brothels are run legally and what it would mean for them if they could operate within the realms of the law. They then take their campaigning for the decriminalisation of brothels to the streets of Sheffield, determined to get the 5,000 signatures they need for an audience with the council(n)

programme.desc: Fizz is at the beach building a sandcastle. Magoo thinks they are preparing to thwart an attack by an enemy riding a dragon.(n)

programme.title: Be Cool, Scooby-Doo!
programme.desc: Monsters and ghouls get in the way of the Scooby gang's adventures as they travel and spend time together in the summer(n)

programme.title: Robozuna
programme.desc: Children's sci-fi fantasy series about 14-year-old orphan Ariston and his robot Mangle, fighting an evil empire together(n)

programme.title: Spy School
programme.desc: The budding spies are called into action against Goldfist at Chelsea Football Club, and uncover a plot with Eden Hazard himself(n)

programme.title: Beautiful Baking with Juliet Sear
programme.desc: Juliet serves up a selection of bakes that involve spices. She makes spiced pumpkin pie with a meringue topping, chocolate and chilli cookies and winter pavlova with spiced apples and pears. She also visits a Lebanese bakery and is joined by food and travel writer John Gregory-Smith, who makes date and cinnamon squares(n)

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner are starting to feel festive with a few winter warmers. Lisa begins with a blue cheese and butternut squash pie, followed by John's sausage casserole. They also serve a family favourite - macaroni cheese, with a twist - and for dessert it's a classic ginger cake served two ways(n)

programme.title: Nadia's Family Feasts
programme.desc: Nadia Sawalha is joined by Asimakis Chaniotis, London's youngest Michelin-starred chef, to create a family feast from Greece. The menu starts with scrambled eggs for breakfast and chicken gyros for lunch. Then Asimakis rustles up his mum's meatball soup for dinner before Nadia makes Greek almond crescent biscuits for dessert(n)

programme.title: How to Spend It Well at Christmas with Phillip Schofield
programme.desc: Phillip Schofield returns to put more Christmas gifts to the test, aided by his wife Steph, celebrity guests and members of the public. They begin by gearing up for Black Friday with tips on the best gifts and gadgets on the market. With sales of personal electric vehicles on the up, Corrie trio Jack AP Shepherd (David), Alan Halsall (Tyrone) and Colson Smith (Craig) put hoverboards and scooters to the test. Clare Balding is on a mission to find the pefect present for pets and Eddie 'the Eagle' Edwards ranks gift experiences such as track racing, skydiving and flyboarding(n)

programme.title: The X Factor: Celebrity
programme.desc: Dermot O'Leary hosts the climax of the star-studded spin-off from the long-running singing contest. Over the past seven weeks, the line-up of famous faces has been whittled down to today's finalists, all under the watchful mentoring of judging panel Simon Cowell, Nicole Scherzinger and Louis Walsh. In this grand final, they will perform to impress both the viewing public and the judges, with the most popular act being crowned as the first celebrity series champion and securing a recording contract(n)

programme.title: Back to Mine
programme.desc: Globetrotting Emma is house hunting for love with the help of her cousin Kelly - and she has a substantial list of demands to be met as she visits three single men's homes. In the running is a rugby player, a trendy musician and a bodybuilder. Which one will she choose to go back to her house for a date?(n)

programme.title: Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows: Part 2 (2011)
programme.desc: The teenage wizard continues his search for the artefacts containing fragments of the dark lord Voldemort's soul. Time is running out, as the evil sorcerer is aware of Harry's mission, and marshals his forces for a final, devastating attack on Harry and his friends at Hogwarts school. Conclusion of the fantasy saga, with Daniel Radcliffe, Ralph Fiennes, Rupert Grint, Emma Watson and Alan Rickman(n)

programme.title: The ITV Election Debate
programme.desc: Julie Etchingham hosts a debate to which seven parties in the General Election campaign have been invited, ahead of the poll on December 12. The parties are the Conservatives, Labour, the Liberal Democrats, the SNP, the Brexit Party, the Greens and Plaid Cymru. Brexit is likely to be high on the agenda for this debate, along with issues including the NHS, crime, education and climate change(n)

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity Get Me Out of Here!
programme.desc: As the campmates face being booted out of the jungle back into their life of luxury, who is ready to make that jump and who wants to stay another day? Presented by Ant and Dec(n)

programme.title: Holiday Horrors: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston narrates a selection of clips submitted by members of the public, which display in cringe-worthy detail some of the many things that can go wrong while away on holiday. A walk in the woods becomes a fight for survival, a man from Wales becomes a shark-wrestler, and an unfortunate holiday-maker ruins all his once-in-a-lifetime holiday snaps(n)

programme.title: Police, Camera, Auction
programme.desc: An insight into the auctions where criminals' assets from their nefarious activities are sold at auction, with the proceeds usually going to help those whose lives have been blighted by crime. From sports cars and designer goods to outrageous bling and strange collectibles, cameras reveal the broad range of criminals' possessions seized by specialist police teams and put under the hammer. The auctioneers and police discuss their work, while those victims who will benefit from the money wait anxiously to see the outcome of the sales(n)

programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home(n)
